**Step 1: Script Writing**  
 Generate a script following the ref, examples, guardrails and guidelines

**Step 2: Image Prompt.** 

1. Generate a base image of your character that will fit this social post video. Prompt this:

   generate a well-detailed description of the character that will fit the video 

| Give me a well-detailed image description of a front view, a direct close-up shot of the character looking directly at the camera. it should be a portrait of him/her like a passport image containing only the close up portrait. |
| :---- |

**Step 3:** **Base Image generation**. 

Use the description of the character that you got to generate the base image for a character that fits the video and with extreme realism, detailed skin, realistic facial imperfections and natural daylight  lighting

**Step 3b: Voice Design**

![][image1]  
Import the base image of the lady and prompt this:

| This is the lady that I am using, and the video is regular-paced. I want a voice tag that will fit her and her age. I am going to use Kling 3.0pro to generate the clips, and I want her to have a consistent voice throughout the video. So give me a voice tag that matches her face and her age. The voice tag should be like a prompt |
| :---- |

The voicetag is basically a description of what the AIGC should sound like to maintain a consistent voice across all AI video clips. This is what the voice tag should look like:

Sample vice tag:

| Voice Tag: A 70-year-old African American woman. Warm, soothing, and trustworthy grandmotherly tone. Slightly raspy texture indicating age, with a rich, resonant lower register. Slow, deliberate pacing with natural, thoughtful pauses. Conversational, intimate, and authentic, as if giving gentle advice to a friend over a cup of tea. |
| :---- |

The voice tag has to be included in every image to video prompts for the A-roll clips to maintain a consistent voice across all clips

**Step 4: Scene and Environment Prompt (Using GPT image 2.0)**

1. Split the script VO dialogue into chunks of 10 \- 20 words per chunk, but make sure not to cut in into sentences.   
     
   It should look something like this:

| Back in the 80s, if you wanted a little something extra ... you had to go out and work for it.  Now I'm retired \- and I'm not looking to pick up another job. But I still like having a little extra here and there. It's funny ... kids nowadays come up with everything. My grandchild showed me this app where you can earn rewards just from using your phone. At first, I didn't really believe it \- but then, I gave it a try. It's called Mode Earn App. You earn points doing what you'd already been doing anyway.  Playing a game. Putting on some music. Reading the news in the morning with my coffee. The points add up quicker than you'd think. And you can trade them in for Amazon gift cards or PayPal. Whatever suits you.I like it because it's easy. It fits into my routine, and I can just use it whenever I feel like it. Nothing complicated, nothing stressful. So if you're going to be on that phone anyway, and Lord knows we all are, you might as well get a little something out of it. Download the Mode Earn app for free now |
| :---- |

   

2. Generate a base environment  image that matches the vibe of the script and would be suitable for the video  
3. Import the character base image and the environment base images to GPT image 2.0  Combine the base image of the environment and the base image of the character and use them to  
   Generate the character in different locations, poses, and camera angles in the same environment for each chunk of the script VO dialogue prompt this:

| Give me a 3 by 3 grid of the character in different positions in the house. She will be in a different sitting position and camera angle. But she will be looking at the camera in all the shots.  |
| :---- |

Do this for each chunk of the Script VO as the images would be used as the first frame to generate the A-roll shots in Kling 3.0 Pro  
The 3x3 grid should look something like this:  
![][image2]

4. Upload the 3x3 grid image to GPT image 2.0 again and prompt it to extract the image that best suits the script chunk from the 3 by 3 grid. Use this prompt

| Extract the image of the lady sitting in the 4th image. |
| :---- |

Save and Rename each image locally  accordingly and immediately after extracting each image for each script chunk  
In this format (img-1 for the image for the first script chunk, img-2 for the second script chunk, etc)  
We generate the character in different location of the base environment to introduce some pattern interruption to the flow

**Step 5**: **Image to Video prompt**

Upload the image that will be converted to a video to kling 3.0pro as first frame and Prompt what the lady is doing and include the scene's camera movement(only static and slightly shaky handheld camera movement. No zoom-in, or any other camera movement allowed), the character's voice tag, and the dialogue chunk.   
**Image to Video clips generation**  
Using each extracted image as the first frame in Kling 3.0 Pro, generate the character talking to the camera using the script VO chunk and the corresponding image for that script VO chunk

I will drop the full clip-by-clip image to video prompt i used to generate the ai clips for each chunk of the back in the 80s video manually, so you could reference this when generating clips

Here are a few samples of what an image to video prompt looks like  
**Kling 3.0 pro First frame image to video prompt example:**

Clip-1  Kling 3.0 pro 7 seconds (20 words dialogue)

| Begin on the exact frontal medium close-up of the 70-year-old African American woman looking directly into the lens, matching her pose with her chin resting gently on her right hand, wearing the patterned cardigan over a grey shirt, with the soft natural window lighting and warm domestic background remaining unchanged. The camera remains completely static with no movement throughout the entire shot. She begins speaking directly to the camera in a warm, soothing, and trustworthy grandmotherly tone with a slightly raspy, rich lower register, delivering the line: "Back in the 80s, if you wanted a little something extra ... you had to go out and work for it." As she speaks with slow, deliberate pacing, she smoothly drops her right hand from her chin and begins performing subtle, natural hand gestures that emphasize her spoken words. Her expressions remain conversational, intimate, and authentically warm, settling into a comfortable, relaxed posture as the spoken line naturally concludes. |
| :---- |

Clip-2 Kling 3.0 pro 8 seconds (23 words dialogue)

| Begin on a medium shot of the 70-year-old African American woman standing slightly further back in a warm, inviting kitchen, looking directly into the lens. The environment features rich wooden cabinets and bright natural light streaming from the window on the left. She begins speaking in a warm, soothing, and trustworthy grandmotherly tone with a slightly raspy, resonant lower register, delivering her lines with slow, deliberate pacing: "Now I'm retired \- and I'm not looking to pick up another job. But I still like having a little extra here and there." As she speaks, she takes a slow, natural step toward the camera, her right hand sliding along the granite countertop for support while her left arm momentarily rests akimbo to match the conversational rhythm of her words. The camera gently adjusts to her forward motion until the shot smoothly resolves into the exact frontal composition shown in the reference. She settles into a comfortable resting pose with her right forearm leaning on the edge of the granite slab, holding a black pen naturally, while her left arm relaxes down by her side. Her authentic, welcoming smile and pose lock perfectly into place, with the lighting, patterned cardigan, and background details remaining completely consistent. |
| :---- |

Clip-3 Kling 3.0 pro 9 seconds (24 words dialogue)

| Begin on the exact frontal medium close-up of the 70-year-old African American woman seated at the wooden table, looking directly into the lens, matching her pose precisely with both elbows resting on the table and her hands clasped beneath her chin, holding a pair of reading glasses. The warm, inviting dining room background with natural window light and wooden chairs remains unchanged. The camera is completely static with no movement throughout the shot. She begins speaking directly to the camera in a warm, soothing, and trustworthy grandmotherly tone with a slightly raspy, resonant lower register, delivering her lines with slow, deliberate pacing: "It's funny ... kids nowadays come up with everything. My grandchild showed me this app where you can earn rewards just from using your phone. " As she speaks, she naturally unclasps her hands and drops them down to rest softly on the wooden table. Midway through her dialogue, in a smooth and conversational motion, she raises her hands again to put on the reading glasses she was holding, settling them comfortably on her face while maintaining unbroken eye contact with the lens. The shot smoothly resolves with her continuing to speak authentically to the camera, now wearing her glasses, her posture relaxed with her hands resting naturally on the table, while the lighting, her patterned cardigan, and all surrounding background details remain completely consistent.  |
| :---- |

Clip-4 Kling 3.0 pro 8 seconds (19 words dialogue)

| Begin on the exact frontal close-up of the 70-year-old African American woman looking directly into the lens with a warm, authentic expression, her hands completely out of frame. Her pose, the patterned cardigan over a grey shirt, and the softly lit domestic background with a bright window on the left remain completely consistent. The camera is locked in a completely static position with zero movement. She begins speaking directly to the camera in a warm, soothing, and trustworthy grandmotherly tone with a slightly raspy, rich lower register, delivering her lines with slow, deliberate pacing: "At first, I didn't really believe it \- but then, I gave it a try. It's called Mode Earn App." As she speaks, she exhibits natural, conversational micro-movements, with gentle nods and subtle shifts in her posture that breathe realism into her delivery. Her facial expressions transition smoothly, reflecting the authentic emotion of her words, before resolving into a bright, welcoming smile. The shot settles perfectly on her warm expression as the line concludes, maintaining the exact framing, lighting, and background continuity throughout. |
| :---- |

Clip-5 Kling 3.0 pro 5 seconds (10 words dialogue)

| Begin on the exact frontal close-up of the 70-year-old African American woman looking directly into the lens with a warm, authentic expression, her hands completely out of frame. Her pose, the patterned cardigan over a grey shirt, and the softly lit domestic background with a bright window on the left remain completely consistent. The camera is locked in a completely static position with zero movement. She begins speaking directly to the camera in a warm, soothing, and trustworthy grandmotherly tone with a slightly raspy, rich lower register, delivering her lines with slow, deliberate pacing: "You earn points doing what you'd already been doing anyway." As she speaks, she exhibits natural, conversational micro-movements, with gentle nods and subtle shifts in her posture that breathe realism into her delivery. Her facial expressions transition smoothly, reflecting the authentic emotion of her words, before resolving into a bright, welcoming smile. The shot settles perfectly on her warm expression as the line concludes, maintaining the exact framing, lighting, and background continuity throughout. |
| :---- |

Clip-6 Kling 3.0 pro 10 seconds (24 words dialogue)

| Begin on the exact wide-medium shot of the 70-year-old African American woman seated comfortably on the cushioned window bench, looking directly into the lens. Her starting pose matches the reference precisely, with her right leg crossed over her left, hands resting naturally clasped on her lap, wearing the patterned cardigan, grey shirt, and blue jeans. The warm yellow walls, hardwood floor, white pillow, and bright natural light streaming from the large window on the right remain completely unchanged. A slight, natural handheld camera movement introduces a gentle, breathing realism to the shot. She begins speaking directly to the camera in a warm, soothing, and trustworthy grandmotherly tone with a slightly raspy, rich lower register, delivering her lines with slow, deliberate pacing: "Playing a game. Putting on some music. Reading the news in the morning with my coffee." Her body exhibits natural, conversational micro-movements, with her facial expressions conveying gentle enthusiasm and authentic reflection. As she delivers the final sentence, "The points add up quicker than you'd think," she smoothly uncrosses her legs in a fluid, natural motion, bringing both legs down while her hands adjust to rest softly on her lap to match the reassuring emotion of her words. The gentle handheld camera drift continues as the shot resolves perfectly with her in this new, open posture. Her warm, welcoming smile remains locked onto the viewer, with the lighting, her clothing, and all background room details staying completely consistent throughout the single, seamless take. |
| :---- |

Clip-7 Kling 3.0 pro 14 seconds (37 words dialogue)

| Begin on the exact frontal medium shot of the 70-year-old African American woman seated comfortably on the beige sofa, looking directly into the lens. Her starting pose matches the reference precisely, holding a rustic ceramic mug with both hands resting near her lap, wearing the patterned cardigan, grey shirt, and blue jeans. The warm, softly lit living room background with light pillows and bright natural light streaming from the window on the left remains completely unchanged. A slight, natural handheld camera movement introduces a gentle, breathing realism to the shot. She begins speaking directly to the camera in a warm, soothing, and trustworthy grandmotherly tone with a slightly raspy, rich lower register, delivering her lines with slow, deliberate pacing: "And you can trade them in for Amazon gift cards or PayPal. Whatever suits you. I like it because it's easy. It fits into my routine, and I can just use it whenever I feel like it." Her facial expressions reflect a relaxed, authentic ease, conveying genuine contentment and reassurance as she speaks. Her body exhibits natural, subtle conversational micro-movements while keeping her hands gently wrapped around the warm mug in her lap. The subtle handheld camera drift continues as the shot resolves perfectly with her settling into a comfortable, authentic smile. Her warm expression remains locked onto the viewer as the dialogue concludes, with the lighting, her clothing, and all background details staying completely consistent throughout the seamless single take. |
| :---- |

Clip-8 Kling 3.0pro 11 seconds (30 words dialogue)

| Begin on the exact frontal medium shot of the 70-year-old African American woman seated comfortably, looking directly into the lens, matching her pose precisely with a small tabby kitten resting on her lap over her blue jeans. She wears the same patterned cardigan and grey shirt, gently petting the cat with her hands. The warm, softly lit living room background with bright natural light streaming from the window on the left remains completely unchanged. A subtle, natural handheld camera movement introduces a gentle, breathing realism to the shot. She begins speaking directly to the camera in a warm, soothing, and trustworthy grandmotherly tone with a slightly raspy, rich lower register, delivering her lines with slow, deliberate pacing: "Nothing complicated, nothing stressful. So if you're going to be on that phone anyway, and Lord knows we all are..." As she speaks, her hands lovingly stroke the cat, which visibly purrs and leans affectionately into her warm touch. As she transitions into her final sentence, "...you might as well get a little something out of it," the cat swiftly but naturally leaps off her legs and out of the lower frame. She reacts with natural, conversational grace, smoothly dropping her hands to rest softly on her lap. The gentle handheld camera drift continues as the shot resolves perfectly with her settling into this new, relaxed posture. Her warm, welcoming smile remains locked onto the viewer as the dialogue concludes, with the lighting, her clothing, and all background details staying completely consistent throughout the seamless single take. |
| :---- |

Clip-9 Kling 3.0pro 3 seconds (8 words dialogue)

| Begin on the exact frontal medium shot of the 70-year-old African American woman seated at the wooden table, looking directly into the lens. Her starting pose matches the reference precisely, with her right arm resting comfortably across her left on the table, while her left hand casually holds a pair of reading glasses. She wears the same patterned cardigan over a grey shirt. The warm, softly lit domestic background remains completely unchanged. A slight, natural handheld camera movement introduces a gentle, breathing realism to the shot. She begins speaking directly to the camera in a warm, soothing, and trustworthy grandmotherly tone with a slightly raspy, rich lower register, delivering her final lines with slow, deliberate pacing: "Download the Mode Earn app for free now.." Her facial expressions convey an inviting, authentic warmth and encouraging reassurance as she speaks. Her body exhibits natural, subtle conversational micro-movements while keeping her arms comfortably positioned on the table. The subtle handheld camera drift continues as the shot resolves perfectly with her settling into a confident, welcoming smile. Her warm expression remains locked onto the viewer as the dialogue concludes, with the lighting, her clothing, and all background details staying completely consistent throughout the seamless single take. |
| :---- |

Note that the amount of words in the dialogue would determine the duration you set for the Kling 3.0 pro image to video gen. Lesser words \= lesser duration, more words \= longer duration

**Step 6**:**Special B-roll clips generation**

Generate specific b-roll shots for some part of the Script as needed. For example, when she mentioned her grandchild in the script, it should be a clip of her grandchild. And when she mentioned the name of the app, it should be a clip of her showing the app UI to the viewer. Her talking head video would still be used as A-roll, but the b-roll clips would be overlayed on top of the talking head A-roll clip  so only the voice over from the talking head is heard while the b-roll is muted but shows on the screen

The Image prompt for the grandchild should look something like this:  
Upload the base image of both the Character and the location and prompt this

| Give me a high-quality, over-the-shoulder photograph taken from just behind the right shoulder of the elderly woman in the patterned cardigan. She occupies the right foreground profile, slightly out of focus. Facing her and smiling happily is a little girl, about 7 years old, her granddaughter, standing on the other side of a coffee table in the sunlit living room. The granddaughter is holding up a distinct black Samsung S22 with its unique triple-camera setup clearly visible on the back, pointing the screen directly towards the woman and the camera. The brightly lit screen is fully visible and in sharp focus, clearly displaying the blue PayPal logo and the exact verification text: "Confirmation \- You've successfully set up your PayPal account" and the green "Continue" button. The natural daylight from the large window illuminates the scene. The atmosphere is warm and joyful. The depth of field is shallow, rendering the foreground woman and background details (like a couch and picture frames) in soft bokeh. Natural, candid, high-detail texture.  |
| :---- |

Then turn it into a video clip with kling 3.0pro and ensure the duration is not longer or shorter than the underlying A-roll clip of the talking character

**Phone UI display**  
For parts where the character is to point the phone to the screen to face the viewers, use Kling 3.0 first frame \+ last frame for this shot  
Generate 2 images for this shot, the first image would be the character in the location holding the phone but not pointing it to viewers yet and the second image would be the character already pointing the phone to the viewer holding up the phone screen very close to viewer as this would be the last frame of the shot  
The last frame image should have the Original App UI or Original App logo from the assets folder. Use GPT image 2.0 to add the Original App logo from the assets to the characters phone before using it as the last frame in the Kling 3.0 first frame \+ last frame shot

The image to video prompt for the App UI display should look something like this-:attach character’s image before pointing phone to camera as first frame and attach character’s image pointing phone facing the viewer close up on phone screen  displaying original App logo/app UI banner from asset folder as last frame

| Make her turn the phone and face it to the screen. The screen of the phone is visible and showing on the phone. The phone is very close to the camera.Begin on the exact frontal medium close-up of the 70-year-old African American woman looking directly into the lens with a warm smile, holding a black android smartphone in her right hand with its back facing the camera. She wears a patterned cardigan over a grey shirt, set against a softly lit domestic background featuring a bright window and delicate string lights. Maintaining her warm expression, she smoothly raises her right hand and rotates the phone forward in a single, fluid motion, turning the device so its bright screen directly faces the camera lens. As she extends the phone closer to the camera to show off the interface, the focus smoothly racks from her face to the device, naturally blurring her smiling face and the room behind her into a soft, warm bokeh. The movement resolves perfectly into a tight, sharp close-up of the smartphone held securely in her hand, clearly displaying a vibrant rewards app interface featuring a "Daily Missions" banner and colorful grid icons, with her out-of-focus silhouette remaining perfectly consistent in the background to complete the seamless single take. |
| :---- |

The image prompt of when the lady mentions “playing a game”: ref images \+

| Give me a low side angle shot of the lady using her phone and smiling. She is using her phone holding it vertically.  |
| :---- |

The image prompt of when the lady mentions “listening to music”: ref images \+

| an (OTSS) over-the-shoulder shot of character. The display on her phone should be a music player. She is playing music on her phone.  She is using her phone holding it vertically.  |
| :---- |

The image prompt of when the lady mentions “reading the news”:

| Give me an over-the-shoulder shot of her. The display on her phone should be news. She is reading the news on her phone.  She is using her phone holding it vertically.  |
| :---- |

Then turn all B-roll images into a video clip with kling 3.0pro and ensure the duration is not longer or shorter than the corresponding A-roll clip of the talking character

We generate and use b-roll clips to introduce some pattern interruption to the flow just like professional social media UGC

[image1]: <data:image/png;base64,>

[image2]: <data:image/png;base64,>
